post.summary:
Admin-only operational endpoint to force-fail a stuck scan (e.g., a scan left Running due to a crash).
This will set Scan.Status=Failed and mark any Pending/Running tools as Failed.

Admin-only: cancel all scans that are Pending or Running.
Marks each scan as Canceled, marks Pending/Running tools as Failed, and cleans up Auth Worker for VPS jobs.

get.summary: Exports scan report as PDF (admin version - any scan).

post.summary:
Admin-only helper to grant a plan to a user (no Stripe).
Useful for support and local testing.

put.summary: Replace entire plan definition by plan name.

get.summary: Get user plan details by ID.
Get current VPS queue with estimated finish times.

delete.summary: Delete a job from the VPS queue.

put.summary: Reorder a job in the VPS queue.

post.summary: Pre-scan: converts a user's intent into a strict scan configuration (AI-powered).
post.description:
Requires PlanId (plans will be enforced soon).
Returns raw AI response plus recommended internal tool names usable by this backend.

post.summary: Post-scan: generates a summarized vulnerability report (AI-powered) for an existing scan.
post.description:
Uses stored, normalized vulnerabilities only (never raw tool output).
Requires PlanId (plans will be enforced soon).

post.summary: Purchase an extra scan credit without Stripe payment (direct purchase for testing/admin).

400.description: Business rule violation (e.g., max 3 extra scans allowed).

404.description: No active plan found. Please purchase a plan first.

post.summary: Purchase or renew a plan without Stripe payment (direct purchase for testing/admin).
Create a new Jira project configuration.

get.summary: Get all Jira projects for the current user.
Get a specific Jira project by ID.

post.summary: Link a Jira project to a target.

delete.summary: Unlink a Jira project from a target.

post.summary: Test Jira connection for a project.

get.summary: Public list of available plans (prices, credits, max runtime, enabled tools).
Returns the current user's active plan + remaining credits.

post.description:
New Format Example (ZAP scan): ``` { "name": "ZAP Scan on Example.com", "scope_signed": true, "targets": ["https://example.com"], "notes": "Baseline scan with AJAX spider", "tool": "zap", "target_config": { "authentication": { "token": "auth-token-here" }, "headers": { "X-Custom-Header": "value" }, "user_agent": "WebAppScanner/2.1" }, "tool_config": { "scan-type": "baseline", "ajax": "true" }, "extra_args": "-d" } ```
New Format Example (ffuf scan): ``` { "name": "Directory Enumeration", "targets": ["https://example.com"], "tool": "ffuf", "tool_config": { "wordlist": "common.txt", "recursion": "true", "mc": "200,301,403", "fc": "404" } } ```
Legacy Format Example: ``` { "targetId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "toolNames": ["SqlInjectionScanner", "XssScanner"] } ```

requestBody.description: Scan creation request. Supports both new format (with Targets and Tool) and legacy format (with TargetId and ToolNames).

202.description: Scan created successfully and queued for processing

400.description: Invalid request (validation error, rate limit exceeded, or target not found)

get.summary: Gets estimated finish time for a scan tool job.
get.description:
Response Fields: queuePosition: Position in queue (0 = currently processing, 1 = next, etc.) jobsAheadCount: Number of jobs ahead expectedFinishTimeSeconds: Sum of estimated durations for all jobs ahead + this job maximumFinishTimeSeconds: Worst-case scenario (all jobs hit their max timeout) estimatedFinishAt: ISO timestamp when job is expected to finish maximumFinishAt: ISO timestamp for worst-case finish time
Usage:
Poll this endpoint every 30 seconds while job is queued. Estimates update automatically as jobs finish.
When you get a 404, the job is no longer in queue - check scan status for results.

404.description: Job not found in queue (may be processing, completed, or failed).

get.summary: Exports scan report as PDF with Arabic support.
get.description:
PDF Report Includes: Scan Information: ID, status, dates (requested, started, finished), duration Summary: Vulnerability counts by severity, total count, risk score Vulnerabilities: Complete details with Arabic support - name, description, severity, CVSS score, affected resources, recommendations Tools: List of scanning tools used with their status and depth
Arabic Support:
The PDF fully supports Arabic text with proper RTL (right-to-left) rendering. All vulnerability descriptions and recommendations in Arabic will be displayed correctly.

post.summary: Create Jira tickets for an existing scan (manual/retroactive ticket creation).
post.description:
This endpoint allows creating Jira tickets for a scan after the report has been generated.
Only vulnerabilities without existing tickets will have tickets created.
Requires a Jira project to be linked to the scan's target.

parameters.name: scanId
parameters.description: The scan ID to create tickets for.

put.summary:
Set browser-based login credentials for a target (for authenticated scanning).
Credentials are encrypted and used by the Auth Worker to perform Playwright login.

delete.summary: Remove browser-based auth configuration from a target.

post.summary:
Receives webhook callbacks from VPS when a job completes.
Authenticates using Bearer token (same token used for VPS API calls).

userId.description: User ID to create scan for. Required - admin must specify which user the scan belongs to.

targetId.description: Legacy: Target ID for backward compatibility. Use Targets array for new format.

toolNames.description: Legacy: Optional explicit list of tools to run. Use Tool for new format.

targets.description: Array of target URLs. Will be normalized and validated.

tool.description: Single tool to run. Supported values: "zap", "ffuf", "sqlmap", "nmap", "xss", "ssl".

extraArgs.description: Extra command-line arguments to pass to the tool.

failureReason.description: Failure reason if status is set to Failed.

planId.description:
Required (plans will be enforced soon).
Example: "free", "pro", "enterprise".

verbosity.description: Optional: "standard" (default) or "high" etc (depends on AI service).

createJiraTickets.description: Optional: Create Jira tickets for vulnerabilities during report generation.

ai.description: Raw upstream AI service JSON (contains status/report/warnings).

jiraTickets.description: Created Jira tickets if CreateJiraTickets was requested.

scanId.description: Optional correlation ID for the scan attempt. If null, the backend will generate one.

planId.description:
Required (plans will be enforced soon).
Example: "free", "pro", "enterprise".

timeBudget.description: Optional time budget string passed through to AI (e.g., "10m", "30m").

ai.description: Raw upstream AI service JSON (contains status/config/clarifying_questions).

recommendedToolNames.description: Recommended internal tool names for this backend (usable in legacy multi-tool scan requests).

targetId.description: Legacy: Target ID for backward compatibility. Use Targets array for new format.

toolNames.description: Legacy: Optional explicit list of tools to run. Use Tool for new format.

targets.description: Array of target URLs. Will be normalized and validated.

tool.description: Single tool to run. Supported values: "zap", "ffuf", "sqlmap", "nmap", "xss", "ssl".

notes.example: Baseline scan with AJAX spider and an auth token.

extraArgs.description: Extra command-line arguments to pass to the tool.

toolDepths.description:
Tool depths configuration: Maps tool names to depth levels.
Keys: Tool names (nmap, zap, ffuf, wpscan, sqlmap)
Values: Depth levels ("light", "deep", "aggressive")
This replaces the simple Tool or ToolNames when depth control is needed.

timeoutMinutes.description:
Optional scan timeout in minutes. When set, overrides the plan's max runtime so the scan can run longer (e.g. 10080 = 7 days).
If not set, the plan's max runtime is used. Capped at 43200 (30 days) when provided.

CreateScanRequest.description:
Request to create a new security scan.
Supports both new format (with Targets array and Tool) and legacy format (with TargetId and ToolNames).

per_scan.description:
Per-scan limits: How many tools of each depth can be used in a single scan.
Used for Basic plan (e.g., max 1 deep tool per scan).
Null means no per-scan limits.

total.description:
Total limits: How many scans of each depth can be used across all scans in plan period.
Used for Professional/Advanced plans (e.g., max 3 deep scans total).
Null means no total limits for that depth.

DepthLimits.description: Depth limits configuration for a plan.

queuePosition.description: Position in queue (0 = currently processing, 1 = next, etc.)

jobsAheadCount.description: Number of jobs that will be processed before this one.

expectedFinishTimeSeconds.description: Sum of estimated durations for all jobs ahead + this job (in seconds).

maximumFinishTimeSeconds.description: Sum of all plan maximum timeouts - worst case scenario (in seconds).

currentJobEstimatedDurationSeconds.description: Estimated duration for this specific job (in seconds).

estimatedFinishAt.description: ISO timestamp when job is expected to finish.

maximumFinishAt.description: ISO timestamp when job will finish at worst (if all jobs hit their max timeout).

jobsAhead.description: List of jobs ahead with their estimated durations and max timeouts.

EstimatedFinishTimeResponse.description: Response containing estimated finish time information for a scan job.

expires_in_months.description: Extra credit expires N months after purchase (default: 2).

min_consumed_percent.description: Only allow buying extra credits when consumed% >= this threshold (default: 80).

max_purchases_per_plan.description: Max times extra credits can be purchased per plan instance (default: 3).

jobId.description: Job ID of the job ahead.

tool.description: Tool name being used by the job.

estimatedDurationSeconds.description: Estimated duration for this job (in seconds).

maxTimeoutSeconds.description: Maximum timeout for this job based on user's plan (in seconds).

JobAheadInfo.description: Information about a job ahead in the queue.

max_runtime_minutes.description: Max runtime per scan (minutes). Must be passed to VPS so it stops at limit.

tools.description: Tool profiles keyed by VPS tool name: zap|nmap|ffuf|sqlmap|wpscan.

expires_in_months.description: Plan expires N months after purchase (default: 6).

allowed_tools.description:
List of allowed tools for this plan. Null means all tools allowed.
Tool names should be VPS tool names: nmap, zap, ffuf, wpscan, sqlmap.

allow_auth_scanning.description: If false, we reject any scan request that includes authentication token.

allow_bruteforce.description: If false, we disallow brute forcing tools (e.g., ffuf) and any explicit brute-force flags.

tool_config.description: Tool-specific config passed to VPS (snake_case keys accepted by VPS).

SetTargetBrowserAuthRequest.description:
Request to set browser-based login credentials for a target.
Username and password are encrypted before storage; never stored in plaintext.

headers.description: Custom HTTP headers to include in requests.

TargetConfigDto.description: Target configuration for authentication, headers, and user agent.

browserAuthConfigured.description: True if browser-based auth (login URL + encrypted credentials) is configured for this target.

inputTokens.description: Number of input tokens consumed (may be null if unavailable).

outputTokens.description: Number of output tokens generated (may be null if unavailable).

totalTokens.description: Total tokens (input + output, may be null if unavailable).

model.description: Model name used for the request (always present).

durationSeconds.description: Duration of the AI service call in seconds (always present).

TokenUsage.description: Token usage information from the AI service.

Bearer.description: JWT Authorization header using the Bearer scheme.
